description:
Patient John Zenon (an industrial maintenance worker) was brought to the clinic after sustaining an accidental 480V electrical shock to his right arm due to a compromised breaker panel. He is conscious and alert but reports muscle spasms and a minor superficial burn on his right hand. He is tachycardic due to the shock and pain.

As the Occupational Health and Safety Technician / Clinician, complete the acute medical documentation in GNU Health for this encounter. Record an active condition for Electrocution/Electric shock (ICD-10 code T75.4 or W86) and a heart rate of 115 bpm in the patient evaluation. Request at least two laboratory tests to monitor for complications (such as Creatine Kinase, Troponin, or a Basic Metabolic Panel) and prescribe an analgesic or topical burn treatment (such as Ibuprofen, Acetaminophen, Naproxen, or Silver Sulfadiazine). Schedule a follow-up appointment for a cardiac rhythm check between 1 and 5 days from today.
